name: Hero Section
description: Full-width hero with badge, headline, description, and CTA buttons
example: Design System Your Product Name A short tagline describing what your product does.
guidelines: Use one solid Button for the primary CTA and outline for secondary
Use Badge above the headline for context labels like version or category
Use Button with asChild to render anchor links while keeping button styles
Keep headline text short — use Text color="secondary" for longer descriptions

name: Feature Grid
description: Grid of Cards with titles and descriptions for showcasing features
example:
Features Everything you need to build great products.
Feature One Description of the first feature. Feature Two Description of the second feature. Feature Three Description of the third feature.
guidelines: Use auto-fit grid with minmax for responsive layouts without media queries
Use Card hoverable prop when cards link to detail pages
Keep card content concise — bold title with secondary-color description
Use Badge inside CardContent for category pills or status indicators

name: Dashboard Layout
description: App shell with Header, sidebar navigation via PageGrid, and main content area
example:
App Name Dashboard Settings
{/* Sidebar */} Navigation {/* Main content */} Dashboard Total Users 1,234 Revenue $12.4k
guidelines: Use Header with sticky for persistent top navigation
Use PageGrid layout="sidebar-left" for sidebar + main content structure
Use Stack for vertical sidebar navigation items and horizontal stat card rows
Metric cards use Card with CardContent — small secondary label above large heading

name: Form Section
description: Form with labeled inputs, select, checkbox, and submit button with validation
example:
<Section size="sm" padding="md">
  <Stack gap="6">
    <Stack gap="2">
      <Heading level={2} size="lg">
        Create Account
      </Heading>
      <Text color="secondary">
        Fill in your details to get started.
      </Text>
    </Stack>

    <Alert variant="error">
      <AlertTitle>Validation Error</AlertTitle>
      <AlertDescription>Please correct the highlighted fields.</AlertDescription>
    </Alert>

    <Stack gap="4">
      <Stack gap="1">
        <Text as="label" size="sm" weight="medium">
          Email
        </Text>
        <Input placeholder="you@example.com" error="Email is required" />
      </Stack>
      <Stack gap="1">
        <Text as="label" size="sm" weight="medium">
          Role
        </Text>
        <Select>
          <SelectTrigger placeholder="Select a role" />
          <SelectContent>
            <SelectItem value="admin">Admin</SelectItem>
            <SelectItem value="editor">Editor</SelectItem>
            <SelectItem value="viewer">Viewer</SelectItem>
          </SelectContent>
        </Select>
      </Stack>
      <Checkbox label="I agree to the terms of service" />
      <Button variant="solid" size="md">Create Account</Button>
    </Stack>
  </Stack>
</Section>
guidelines: Use Text as="label" with size="sm" and weight="medium" for field labels
Use Input error prop with a string to show inline validation messages
Use Stack gap="4" to space form fields evenly
Place Alert variant="error" above the form to show summary validation errors
Always pair Checkbox with a descriptive label prop

name: Dialog with Form
description: Modal dialog containing form fields with cancel and submit actions in the footer
example:
<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="solid">Edit Profile</Button>
  </DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Edit Profile</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>
        Update your display name and email address.
      </DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>

    <Stack gap="4" style={{ padding: 'var(--stella-spacing-4) 0' }}>
      <Stack gap="1">
        <Text as="label" size="sm" weight="medium">
          Display Name
        </Text>
        <Input placeholder="Jane Doe" />
      </Stack>
      <Stack gap="1">
        <Text as="label" size="sm" weight="medium">
          Email
        </Text>
        <Input placeholder="jane@example.com" />
      </Stack>
    </Stack>

    <DialogFooter>
      <DialogClose asChild>
        <Button variant="ghost" size="sm">Cancel</Button>
      </DialogClose>
      <Button variant="solid" size="sm">Save Changes</Button>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>
guidelines: Always include DialogHeader with DialogTitle for accessibility
Use DialogDescription to explain the purpose of the dialog
Use DialogClose asChild on the cancel button to auto-close the dialog
Keep the submit button as a regular Button (not DialogClose) to allow validation before closing
Use Stack gap="4" for form field spacing inside the dialog body

name: Content Page
description: Documentation-style page with breadcrumb navigation, heading, body text, and related links
example:
Docs Guides Getting Started Guide Getting Started Learn how to set up and start using the design system. Body content goes here. Use Text components for paragraphs. Next Steps
Components Browse all available components Tokens Explore the design token system
guidelines: Use Breadcrumb at the top for hierarchical navigation context
Mark the last breadcrumb item with isCurrentPage to render as non-interactive text
Use Badge above the heading for page category or document type
Use Separator to visually divide the header from body content
Use Button variant="ghost" with asChild for prev/next page navigation links

name: Settings Page
description: Tabbed settings page with form sections inside each panel
example:
<Section size="md" padding="md">
  <Stack gap="6">
    <Stack gap="2">
      <Heading level={1} size="xl">
        Settings
      </Heading>
      <Text color="secondary">
        Manage your account preferences.
      </Text>
    </Stack>

    <Tabs defaultValue="profile" variant="line">
      <TabsList>
        <TabsTrigger value="profile">Profile</TabsTrigger>
        <TabsTrigger value="notifications">Notifications</TabsTrigger>
        <TabsTrigger value="appearance">Appearance</TabsTrigger>
      </TabsList>

      <TabsContent value="profile">
        <Stack gap="4" style={{ paddingTop: 'var(--stella-spacing-6)' }}>
          <Stack gap="1">
            <Text as="label" size="sm" weight="medium">
              Display Name
            </Text>
            <Input placeholder="Your name" />
          </Stack>
          <Stack gap="1">
            <Text as="label" size="sm" weight="medium">
              Email
            </Text>
            <Input placeholder="you@example.com" />
          </Stack>
          <Separator />
          <Button variant="solid" size="md">Save Profile</Button>
        </Stack>
      </TabsContent>

      <TabsContent value="notifications">
        <Stack gap="4" style={{ paddingTop: 'var(--stella-spacing-6)' }}>
          <Switch label="Email notifications" defaultChecked />
          <Switch label="Push notifications" />
          <Switch label="Marketing emails" />
          <Separator />
          <Button variant="solid" size="md">Save Preferences</Button>
        </Stack>
      </TabsContent>

      <TabsContent value="appearance">
        <Stack gap="4" style={{ paddingTop: 'var(--stella-spacing-6)' }}>
          <Stack gap="1">
            <Text as="label" size="sm" weight="medium">
              Theme
            </Text>
            <Select>
              <SelectTrigger placeholder="Choose theme" />
              <SelectContent>
                <SelectItem value="dark">Dark</SelectItem>
                <SelectItem value="light">Light</SelectItem>
                <SelectItem value="system">System</SelectItem>
              </SelectContent>
            </Select>
          </Stack>
          <Separator />
          <Button variant="solid" size="md">Save Appearance</Button>
        </Stack>
      </TabsContent>
    </Tabs>
  </Stack>
</Section>
guidelines: Use Tabs variant="line" for settings pages and content-level tabs — solid variant is for navigation-level tabs
Each TabsContent panel should be self-contained with its own save button
Use Separator before the save button to visually separate actions from fields
Use Switch for boolean preferences and Select for enumerated choices
Stack gap="4" provides consistent spacing between form fields in each panel
